name: Orange Cloud Cakes with Boysenberry Sauce
recipeIngredient: Frozen Boysenberries (2 cups), thawed and undrained
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine boysenberries and sugar in a food processor. Process until pureed.
2. Strain to remove seeds. Store prepared sauce up to 3 days ahead; chill.
3. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease 6 custard cups or souffle dishes with vegetable shortening.
4. Sift whole wheat flour, all-purpose flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt into a medium bowl.
5. Whisk egg yolk, nonfat milk, vegetable oil, orange peel, and vanilla extract in a small bowl to blend.
6. Scrape seeds from vanilla bean into the egg yolk mixture.
7. Add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients and mix until just combined.
8. Using an electric mixer, beat egg whites with powdered sugar until stiff but not dry.
9. Fold the beaten egg whites into the batter.
10. Divide the batter among the prepared cups.
11. Bake until golden brown and a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean, about 20 minutes.
12. Cool on a rack. Prepare up to 4 hours ahead.
13. Using a small knife, cut between cakes and cups to loosen. Transfer cakes to plates.
14. Serve with sauce.
